text:
Law firms that track marketing ROI down to the case close 3x their revenue from the same ad spend.

Most firms are flying blind. That gap is the opportunity.

hugheyllc.com

#LawFirmMarketing #LegalMarketing

Your agency sends you a monthly report full of impressions and clicks.

But can they tell you how many clients those clicks became?

If not, you're not measuring marketing. You're measuring activity.

#LegalMarketing #LawFirm

The #1 mistake small law firms make: hiring a generalist marketing agency.

Legal marketing is different. The buyer journey is different. The compliance requirements are different.

Specialization wins every time.

hugheyllc.com

Quick test for your law firm:

Do you know your cost per acquired client by practice area?

If not, you can't make smart marketing decisions — you're just guessing.

We fix that. → hugheyllc.com

#LawFirmMarketing

Most law firm websites are built to impress other lawyers.

They should be built to convert anxious people searching for help at 11pm.

Big difference.

#LegalMarketing #LawFirmWebsite

Google Ads for law firms: the firms winning aren't spending more.

They're tracking which keywords actually produce clients — not just clicks.

Data beats budget.

hugheyllc.com

#LawFirmMarketing #PPC

A law firm client once told me: 'Our agency says the campaign is performing great.'

I asked: how many clients did it generate this month?

They didn't know. Neither did the agency.

That's the problem we solve.

hugheyllc.com

SEO for law firms in 2025 isn't just Google anymore.

It's showing up in AI answers — ChatGPT, Perplexity, Gemini.

If you're not optimizing for AI search, your competitors will be.

#LegalSEO #AEO #LawFirmMarketing

The best marketing move a small law firm can make:

Owninga niche. 'Personal injury attorney' is a war.

'Tampa motorcycle accident attorney' is a winnable fight.

Narrow your focus. Own your market.

#LegalMarketing

Law firm intake is a marketing problem.

You can run perfect ads, rank #1 on Google, and still lose clients because someone answered the phone badly.

Marketing doesn't end at the click.

hugheyllc.com

#LawFirmMarketing

Most law firms have 3-5 reviews on Google.

Their top competitor has 200+.

Reviews are the most underinvested asset in legal marketing. Easy to fix. Hard to ignore.

#LegalMarketing #LawFirm

If your law firm's marketing strategy is 'we have a website and run some ads' — you don't have a strategy.

You have a presence. That's not enough in 2025.

hugheyllc.com

#LawFirmMarketing

Law firms that blog consistently get 55% more website visitors.

Most firms know this. Most firms still don't do it.

Execution is the strategy.

#LegalMarketing #ContentMarketing

Referrals are great. But you can't scale a law firm on referrals alone.

The firms growing past $2M are building digital pipelines that run 24/7 — not just waiting for the phone to ring.

hugheyllc.com

What a $3,000/month law firm marketing agency should deliver:

→ Monthly new client attribution report
→ Cost per lead by channel
→ Conversion rate from lead to retained client
→ Clear next 30-day action plan

Are you getting this? If not, let's talk. hugheyllc.com

The firms that will dominate legal marketing in the next 3 years:

→ Own their local Google presence
→ Show up in AI search results
→ Have a real intake system
→ Track ROI at the case level

All of this is buildable today.

hugheyllc.com

#LegalMarketing

Hot take: most law firm 'marketing problems' are actually sales problems.

Great leads. Slow follow-up. Lost clients.

Fix your intake before you spend another dollar on ads.

#LawFirm #LegalMarketing

Your Google Business Profile is free real estate.

Most law firms treat it like an afterthought.

Complete profile + consistent reviews + weekly posts = more calls with $0 in ad spend.

hugheyllc.com

#LocalSEO #LawFirmMarketing

I've audited 100+ law firm marketing setups.

The most common problem: paying for traffic they can't convert.

Fix the website and intake first. Then scale the ads.

hugheyllc.com

#LawFirmMarketing

The best law firm websites do one thing well:

Make a scared, confused person feel like they've found the right attorney.

Credibility. Clarity. Easy next step.

Everything else is noise.

hugheyllc.com
